page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.13xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 56 dedicated physical cores and 56 vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 224.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the weak category, with single-threaded gzip compression rated as poor. However, the high core count compensates in parallelized tasks. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core scores, consistently ranks in the average tier. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks, along with Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput, also demonstrate average tier performance.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.sn2.13xlarge offers a high density of dedicated physical cores, making it suitable for multi-threaded workloads that can distribute execution across 56 cores. The primary tradeoff is the lower single-thread efficiency, which limits its suitability for single-threaded or latency-sensitive applications. Given the balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio and average multi-core and database benchmark results, this instance is appropriate for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.13xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 56 dedicated physical cores and 56 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor at 2.5 GHz. It is configured with 224.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks reveal a tradeoff: single-core and single-threaded operations, such as gzip compression, perform in the weak to poor tiers, whereas multi-core and multi-threaded operations, including PassMark CPU, Geekbench multi-core, and Redis SET operations, consistently achieve average tier results. This resource profile and performance distribution make the instance suitable for parallelized workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations that benefit from high core counts rather than high single-thread speed.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.13xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 56 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 224 GB RAM, and 10 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core and weak single-core performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2.13xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 56 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 224 GB RAM, and 10 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: A dedicated 56-core x86_64 virtual server with balanced memory allocation, optimized for multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 56 dedicated Intel Xeon physical cores with 224 GB of system memory
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU accelerators
Average multi-core performance across Geekbench and PassMark benchmarks
Weak single-core and single-threaded performance limits latency-sensitive tasks
Appropriate for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, and database operations
